title: Boy Meets Curl

jokes.text: Bart, you tell me this dress doesn't make me look fat. And now! (mist spritzing) you don't look fat.
jokes.setup: Marge asks Bart to tell her the dress doesn't make her look fat
jokes.punchline: Bart takes the instruction literally and says it only after she sprays perfume
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's literal interpretation and the absurdity of date night preparation rituals

jokes.text: (spritzing) homer, you don't look fat. (chuckling): Oh, carl! You're a liar, but I love it.
jokes.setup: Bart continues the perfume-triggered compliment pattern
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks it's Carl complimenting him and appreciates the lie
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Homer's obliviousness and appreciation for flattery even when acknowledged as lies

jokes.text: Date night: It's the embalming fluid That keeps the mummy of a marriage fresh After the heart and brain have been Pulled out through the nose.
jokes.setup: Describing date night as preservative for marriage
jokes.punchline: Elaborate mummy metaphor suggesting marriage is dead but preserved
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and original metaphor that's both funny and insightful about long marriages

jokes.text: Ugh, I never should've given you that egyptology book.
jokes.setup: Marge's elaborate Egyptian metaphor
jokes.punchline: Homer regrets giving her educational material that led to this observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response showing his regret at accidentally making Marge more articulate about their problems

jokes.text: Tonight I will be continuing my courtship Of princess wilhelmina of prussia. Tonight's the night I pop the question: Where is the kaiser's gold?
jokes.setup: Burns announcing he'll pop the question to a princess
jokes.punchline: The question is about Kaiser's gold, not marriage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns joke - combines his age, greed, and romantic delusions with great misdirection

jokes.text: I had one of my boobs reduced. You're one smart cookie, rosemary. That left boob was too good for him.
jokes.setup: Woman explaining how she showed up a man who stood her up
jokes.punchline: Reducing one breast as revenge, with friend's supportive response
jokes.explanation: Absurd TV parody that's deliberately over-the-top but lands through pure bizarreness

jokes.text: How romantic? It'll make a walk on the beach Seem like a punch in the face.
jokes.setup: Marge asking how romantic the movie will be
jokes.punchline: Homer's backwards comparison making romance sound violent
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - trying to be romantic but accidentally making it sound awful

jokes.text: Did you call the furnace man? You said you were gonna call him! He's your cousin!
jokes.setup: Simple question about calling the furnace man
jokes.punchline: Escalating accusations ending with the family connection making it worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how small household issues spiral in marriages

jokes.text: it's so noisy in here. I can't hear the ad telling everyone to be quiet!
jokes.setup: Complaining about noise in movie theater
jokes.punchline: Can't hear the anti-noise ad because of noise
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic observation about the futility of movie theater courtesy announcements

jokes.text: How about bromance? It's not the same. Dude... I'm not a dude, I'm a hottie. This bromance just got interesting.
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting bromance as substitute for romance
jokes.punchline: Marge's assertion she's a hottie making Homer interested in this 'bromance'
jokes.explanation: Playful gender confusion joke that works through Homer's misunderstanding of the term

jokes.text: A sport that encourages hand-holding. Would you like to wear mittens or go commando?
jokes.setup: Homer appreciating ice skating for the hand-holding
jokes.punchline: Offering mittens or 'going commando' for hand-holding
jokes.explanation: Mild sexual innuendo that works through Homer's romantic cluelessness

jokes.text: What, they come here on Saturday night to clean the ice? Losers.
jokes.setup: Homer sees people at ice rink on Saturday night
jokes.punchline: Homer calls them losers while he's also there on Saturday night
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness - criticizing others for the same thing he's doing

jokes.text: Marge, this is perfect for both of us! It's got bowling for me and sweeping for you.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why curling is perfect
jokes.punchline: Reducing it to gender stereotypes - sports for him, cleaning for her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - finding the most reductive way to understand a sport while being accidentally honest about their dynamic

jokes.text: Let us curl, milady. Let us throw and sweep a'twain Until the heavens themselves Drop their jaws in wonder and envy!
jokes.setup: Overly dramatic invitation to try curling
jokes.punchline: Shakespearean language applied to mundane sport
jokes.explanation: Amusing overstatement that parodies overly passionate sports enthusiasm

jokes.text: And afterwards there'll be beer and cocoa, With marshmallows floating in the foam.
jokes.setup: Continuing the romantic curling invitation
jokes.punchline: Bizarre combination of beer and cocoa with marshmallows
jokes.explanation: Delightfully weird image that captures Canadian winter culture through American eyes

jokes.text: This is what you're like when you don't drink!
jokes.setup: Homer's enthusiastic romantic speech about curling
jokes.punchline: Marge realizing this passion comes from sobriety
jokes.explanation: Perfect character insight - Marge discovering Homer's hidden romantic side only emerges when sober

jokes.text: Where'd you learn to sweep like that? I've been training all my life. I once swept red wine off a white carpet.
jokes.setup: Impressed question about Marge's sweeping skills
jokes.punchline: Her 'training' is just being a housewife dealing with stains
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing how Marge's domestic skills translate to athletic prowess

jokes.text: I like your style, blue. Want to join our team? If homer can join with me. Fine. You two are off the team.
jokes.setup: Invitation to join team with Homer as condition
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal kicking them both off
jokes.explanation: Sharp reversal joke with perfect economy showing Homer as unwanted package deal

jokes.text: So, we'd just thrown a biter, And homer said to kick harder off the hack. So I did and I almost fouled the hog line!
jokes.setup: Marge excitedly explaining curling technique
jokes.punchline: String of curling jargon that sounds sexual/nonsensical
jokes.explanation: Great use of real curling terminology that sounds absurd to non-players, plus Marge's enthusiasm

jokes.text: I just have one question: Is curling a real thing? Yeah, or is it just a cover story for a grown-up thing We're not allowed to know about?
jokes.setup: Kids questioning if curling is real
jokes.punchline: Suspecting it's cover for adult activities they can't know about
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke playing on how bizarre curling seems to outsiders, with kid logic

jokes.text: Yeah, like the time you said Dad was taking a weekend leadership seminar, When he was really stuck in a barrel at the junkyard?
jokes.setup: Examples of parental cover stories
jokes.punchline: Homer's 'seminar' was actually being trapped in junkyard barrel
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd detail that's perfectly Homer - both the getting trapped and the elaborate cover story

jokes.text: Principal skinner?! This is bogus, man. You know the rules: Two letters and a conference Before I get a home visit.
jokes.setup: Bart seeing Principal Skinner at the door
jokes.punchline: Knowing the exact protocol for disciplinary escalation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - he knows the school disciplinary system better than most procedures

jokes.text: Bart, my cargo pants indicate That I'm not here on school business. I'm here on 'cool' business, I.E. Curling.
jokes.setup: Skinner explaining he's not here professionally
jokes.punchline: Cargo pants as indicator of casual/cool business, which is curling
jokes.explanation: Great Skinner characterization - his idea of 'cool' clothes and 'cool' activities are wonderfully nerdy

jokes.text: There's a winter olympics?
jokes.setup: Announcement about Winter Olympics curling event
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete ignorance of Winter Olympics existence
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer ignorance delivered perfectly - not knowing about major international event

jokes.text: He usually stays out like this for about 30 seconds. Want some coffee?
jokes.setup: Homer fainting from Olympic news
jokes.punchline: Marge casually timing his unconsciousness and offering coffee
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - practical response to Homer's dramatics, showing this happens regularly

jokes.text: Terrible, seymour! You're a disgrace to frozen water!
jokes.setup: Agnes critiquing Skinner's curling
jokes.punchline: Escalating to being a disgrace to the element itself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Agnes insult - taking criticism to absurd extreme while staying in character

jokes.text: It was a 50's style romance-- Three minutes of sex and a lifetime of regret.
jokes.setup: Agnes describing her 1950s romance
jokes.punchline: Brief pleasure followed by permanent consequences
jokes.explanation: Brutally efficient dark comedy that captures both the era and Agnes's bitterness perfectly

jokes.text: The whole nine months, Seymour had just laid there like a lump. Then this pre-natal nitwit Decides to do his rockettes impression.
jokes.setup: Agnes explaining how Seymour ruined her Olympics
jokes.punchline: Blaming fetal Seymour for kicking and ruining her pole vaulting
jokes.explanation: Hilariously absurd blame assignment and the image of fetal Rockettes dancing

jokes.text: Yes, that involuntary pre-conscious reflex I made in utero was unforgivable.
jokes.setup: Agnes blaming fetal Seymour
jokes.punchline: Skinner's overly technical, sarcastic apology for being born
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner response - academic language applied to absurd maternal blame

jokes.text: They don't give out medals for being a loving wife. Well, they should. Well, they don't!
jokes.setup: Agnes explaining her harsh philosophy
jokes.punchline: Shooting down Marge's hopeful suggestion with finality
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between Marge's optimism and Agnes's harsh realism

jokes.text: Oh, thanks! That's chilbert. One of the four winter games mascots. Would you like it? I've got doubles.
jokes.setup: Friendly pin collector offering information
jokes.punchline: Casually having duplicates of rare Olympic pins
jokes.explanation: Sets up addiction storyline while showing collector mentality of having extras

jokes.text: Just beware, collecting olympic pins can be kind of addictive. (chuckles) come on, they're just pins.
jokes.setup: Warning about pin collecting addiction
jokes.punchline: Lisa dismissing the warning that will prove prophetic
jokes.explanation: Standard addiction setup with dramatic irony for viewers who know Lisa will get hooked

jokes.text: He does look lonely all by himself. (gasps) oh! Buy us! Buy us with money!
jokes.setup: Lisa looking at single pin sympathetically
jokes.punchline: Imagining pins begging to be purchased
jokes.explanation: Good addiction progression showing Lisa's rationalization turning into hallucination

jokes.text: (hoarse male voice): Please let me go back to the cruise ship. I was chief purser. Never! Now get back in character, monsieur wee wee.
jokes.setup: Cute Olympic mascots performing
jokes.punchline: Performer desperately wanting to escape to his real job
jokes.explanation: Dark peek behind Olympic pageantry showing the human cost of cheerful mascot work

jokes.text: Someday I'll be the tall guy.
jokes.setup: Mascot performer's earlier desperation
jokes.punchline: His modest career aspiration within the mascot hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Sad-funny moment showing how low his career aspirations have become

jokes.text: That stone is coming in like a rocket. It's gotta be going four, maybe five miles an hour!
jokes.setup: Dramatic sports commentary building tension
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic speed that's absurdly slow for 'rocket' comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of sports commentary hyperbole applied to curling's leisurely pace

jokes.text: Someone needs to convert that kinetic energy back into potential.
jokes.setup: Tense curling moment needing intervention
jokes.punchline: Lisa applying physics terminology to sports strategy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - understanding sports through scientific principles rather than athletics

jokes.text: Pack your winter coat. We're going to canada's warmest city!
jokes.setup: Celebrating trip to Vancouver
jokes.punchline: Needing winter coat for Canada's 'warmest' city
jokes.explanation: Geographic humor about Canadian climate that works through American perspective

jokes.text: She used to be my therapist. How can I ever thank you? Pay your bill! I couldn't hear the last thing you said! Good-bye forever!
jokes.setup: Homer gratefully encountering his former therapist
jokes.punchline: She wants payment, he conveniently can't hear, permanent goodbye
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Homer's selective hearing when it comes to responsibility

jokes.text: Who you gonna call? Albania! Algeria! Argentina! (wearily): Zimbabwe.
jokes.setup: Olympic ceremony using Ghostbusters reference for each country
jokes.punchline: Director's exhaustion by the end of alphabetical list
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke showing the tedium of Olympic ceremony spectacle

jokes.text: And now, in the olympic spirit of peace and goodwill, We release the canadian dove, The beaver!
jokes.setup: Traditional Olympic dove release ceremony
jokes.punchline: Canadian version uses beaver instead of dove
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural substitution that parodies Olympic ceremony pomposity

jokes.text: This was all preventable!
jokes.setup: Beaver release going wrong (implied chaos)
jokes.punchline: Dramatic reaction as if it were a tragedy
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing broadcaster's tendency to dramatize even silly mishaps

jokes.text: Hey, bort, your sister's hotter than a calgary brushfire. Mind if I ask her oot?
jokes.setup: Canadian version of Milhouse hitting on Lisa
jokes.punchline: Canadian accent and regional reference for pickup line
jokes.explanation: Standard cultural stereotype humor elevated by specific local reference

jokes.text: She's as loony as a one-dollar coin.
jokes.setup: Describing Lisa's pin obsession
jokes.punchline: Canadian 'loony' coin reference replacing standard 'loony' phrase
jokes.explanation: Clever Canadian currency wordplay that works both literally and figuratively

jokes.text: ¶ homer simpson wins a gold, sliding rocks on ice, bao bao! ¶
jokes.setup: Homer singing victory song
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical lyrics mixing Old MacDonald tune with curling and random 'bao bao'
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer nonsense song that captures his simple joy and confused cultural mixing

jokes.text: But as an olympic curler, he's dragging us down Like an anchor on a bag of toasters.
jokes.setup: Criticizing Homer's curling ability
jokes.punchline: Absurd double-weight metaphor of anchor plus toasters
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully excessive metaphor that escalates beyond necessary for comedic effect

jokes.text: I had one olympics ruined by a bald, drooling idiot, But it's not gonna happen again. I was not bald. My fontanel was covered by lanugo.
jokes.setup: Agnes referring to previous Olympic disappointment
jokes.punchline: Skinner's technical correction about his fetal hair coverage
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with Skinner's obsessive technical correction about his prenatal condition

jokes.text: The hat makes me the coach.
jokes.setup: Question about who made her coach
jokes.punchline: Simple, arbitrary authority based on costume accessory
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective joke about arbitrary authority symbols in amateur sports

jokes.text: It's ennui, himself. I must have him. Can I have it on account? On account of I want it? No, it's just not funny the first time.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to get pin with bad wordplay
jokes.punchline: Dealer's honest assessment of the unfunny joke
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about bad jokes not improving with repetition, plus addiction desperation

jokes.text: These were a gift from my mom When I could first read at a twelfth grade level.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining the significance of her pearls
jokes.punchline: Academic achievement milestone instead of normal childhood marker
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa characterization - her childhood milestones are intellectual rather than physical

jokes.text: A dime?! What do you think I am- a payphone from 1980?!
jokes.setup: Someone offering a dime for Lisa's music
jokes.punchline: Indignant comparison to obsolete technology pricing
jokes.explanation: Good reference humor showing Lisa's desperation has made her aggressive about money

jokes.text: There is no dress. It's just pins.
jokes.setup: Bart noticing noise from Lisa's pin-covered dress
jokes.punchline: Revealing the dress is entirely made of pins
jokes.explanation: Perfect addiction escalation visual - complete replacement of clothing with obsession object

jokes.text: Oh, my, that delivery has less juice than sunny delight.
jokes.setup: Weak curling stone delivery
jokes.punchline: Comparing to notoriously weak juice drink
jokes.explanation: Decent brand reference humor that works through shared knowledge of product weakness

jokes.text: She pulled off an impossible move, Just like the time I shaved off larry bird's moustache While he was napping.
jokes.setup: Praising Marge's impossible curling move
jokes.punchline: Comparing to completely unrelated, bizarre personal anecdote
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random Costas anecdote that parodies his tendency toward personal sports stories

jokes.text: This is the sort of bittersweet melodrama Olympic coverage feeds on. I admit it. We're vampires who suck on shattered dreams.
jokes.setup: Marge getting injured during victory
jokes.punchline: Costas admitting Olympic coverage exploits athlete suffering
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on Olympic broadcasting that's both funny and genuinely insightful

jokes.text: Ooh, you're hot when you're horny. I'm not horny. I'm mad. Ooh, you're mad when you're angry.
jokes.setup: Homer misinterpreting Marge's emotion
jokes.punchline: Double misunderstanding of 'horny' then redundant 'mad when angry'
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer sexual misunderstanding followed by redundant observation

jokes.text: I should be. I'm left-handed. I've been using my right hand all these years 'cause I didn't want to seem unusual in any way.
jokes.setup: Compliment about Marge's left-hand dexterity
jokes.punchline: Revealing she's naturally left-handed but hid it for conformity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge characterization - suppressing natural ability to avoid standing out

jokes.text: The first thing you have to do is get clean, so give me your pins. All of 'em. What is this, tough love? Uh, it's a little more like soft hate.
jokes.setup: Bart helping Lisa with pin addiction intervention
jokes.punchline: Reframing 'tough love' as 'soft hate'
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that captures sibling relationship - caring but reluctantly

jokes.text: Without them, I'm just a big maggie.
jokes.setup: Lisa devastated about losing her pearls
jokes.punchline: Defining herself as just a larger version of baby Maggie without pearls
jokes.explanation: Brilliant insight about Lisa's identity being tied to her intellectual accessories

jokes.text: His name is fatov. He represents the russian spirit of sloth and alcoholism.
jokes.setup: Bart offering rare Russian Olympic pin
jokes.punchline: Mascot representing negative Russian stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny mascot that parodies Olympic idealism with honest national stereotypes

jokes.text: Let's give the olympics a miracle. But this time, on ice.
jokes.setup: Team giving up in defeat
jokes.punchline: Homer's inspiring speech references 'Miracle on Ice' hockey game
jokes.explanation: Decent sports reference with Homer trying to inspire through pop culture

jokes.text: Ah, they shouldn't be playing quidditch at all. Not while four of the seven horcruxes are still unfound.
jokes.setup: Watching Olympic curling
jokes.punchline: Confusing it with Harry Potter and applying wizarding world logic
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference humor that works through the absurdity of confused fandoms

jokes.text: Before now, I never knew a mother could love her child. Win or lose, I'm taking you out for pizza and ice cream, And we can sit at the same table.
jokes.setup: Agnes showing rare maternal affection
jokes.punchline: The 'special treat' is basic human dignity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy showing how low Agnes's parenting standards are

jokes.text: Before now, I never knew a mother could love her child. Win or lose, I'm taking you out for pizza and ice cream, And we can sit at the same table.
jokes.setup: Agnes showing rare maternal affection
jokes.punchline: The 'generous' offer to eat at the same table
jokes.explanation: Perfect Agnes moment - her idea of maternal generosity is pathetically minimal

jokes.text: Kids, open your history books, tear out the pages And put this indelible olympic moment in.
jokes.setup: Dramatic call to remember historic Olympic moment
jokes.punchline: Literally destroying textbooks to insert curling victory
jokes.explanation: Absurd over-dramatization of minor Olympic demonstration sport victory

jokes.text: Take that, sweden! Tell me how my ice tastes.
jokes.setup: Homer celebrating victory over Sweden
jokes.punchline: Bizarre taunt about ice tasting
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer victory celebration with nonsensical sports smack talk

tmdb_overview: Homer takes Marge out for a romantic evening of ice skating and hand-holding, but upon entering the rink, they encounter a curling team practising. Marge and Homer take to the ice and discover their love for the sport, and soon after, join the curling team and compete with them in the Olympic trials. Team Springfield claims the win and moves on to the 2010 Vancouver Games where Bob Costas covers the action. Meanwhile, sleazy vendors introduce Lisa to the world of collecting Olympic pins, and before long, Lisa is hopelessly addicted.
